text: Barter is a way people exchange goods or services without using money. text: Abiotic stress happens when living things are affected by non-living factors in their environment. Too much or too little light also affects how well plants make food through photosynthesis. Abiotic stress is a big problem because it hurts crop yields, kills plants, and disrupts entire ecosystems. This cycle of making spores and growing new plants is called alternation of generations. text: Amdahl's law is a way to estimate how much faster a computer program can run if it uses many processors at the same time. Gene Amdahl came up with this idea in 1967 and found that there's a limit to how fast a program can go, even if most of it can be split into smaller tasks that can be done by multiple processors. This is because some parts of the program have to run one after the other, which slows everything down. Amdahl's law says that no matter how many processors you add, there will always be a part of the program that can't be sped up much more. The law uses numbers and formulas to show this idea, and it helps programmers understand why their programs might not get as fast as they want them to, even with lots of powerful computers working together.

It gets inside cells by using the same transport system that brings in other important ions like sulfate and phosphate. Chromium(VI)'s strong oxidizing properties make it especially hazardous to the kidneys, liver, and blood cells. If ingested, it can lead to cell damage, kidney failure, and liver problems. In severe cases, dialysis may be necessary to help remove the toxin from the body.

text: A zero-suppressed decision diagram is a way to represent and work with complex computer programs using true or false values. This helps when dealing with big digital circuits, checking models, and other tasks in computer-aided design where complex programs need to be stored and worked with efficiently. The zero-suppressed decision diagram is built similarly to a binary decision diagram but includes an extra flag that says whether each node's value will always be false, allowing it to remove those nodes and make the program more compact. text: The Brouwer fixed-point theorem is an important result in mathematics that says any continuous function from a closed ball to itself will always have at least one point that stays the same. Over time, other mathematicians have built upon Brouwer's idea, but its core remains a fundamental principle in mathematics.

A special version that can carry both passengers and cargo, called a combi, began service with KLM in September 1989. The freight-only version entered service with Cargolux in November 1993. Later, Boeing modified some of the passenger planes to make them suitable for carrying cargo only. This was done for Air France in October 2002 and Qantas in November 2002. In 2004, Boeing started a program with Cathay Pacific to convert more passenger planes into freighters, which they called the Boeing Converted Freighter. The first plane modified under this program was delivered in December 2005.
label: human

text: A domain can be divided into smaller groups called organizational units (OUs). These OUs help organize and manage a domain by creating a hierarchy, making it easier to administer. They can also reflect the organization's structure in terms of management or geography. An OU can contain other OUs, just like how a container holds objects inside. Microsoft suggests using OUs instead of domains for organizing and simplifying policy implementation. The OU is where group policies are typically applied, which are settings that control user access and behavior. These policies can also be set at the domain or site level. Administrative powers are often delegated to OUs, but they can also be given to individual objects or attributes if needed.
label: human

text: The Great Western Railway's 4073 Class is a group of steam trains designed by Charles Collett in the early 1930s. One of these trains is called 7027 Thornbury Castle, which was built at Swindon Works in 1935 and originally had the number 4073. The train was named after a historic house near Bristol. In its working days, 7027 traveled on many routes across the GWR network, including fast passenger trains between London and the West Country. After the railways were taken over by the government in 1948, the train continued to run under British Railways until it stopped working in 1962. Later, a private owner restored it to working order and now it's owned by the West Country Railway Association. The train is used for special events and excursions throughout the year, giving people a chance to see and hear what a classic steam train sounds like.
label: llm

text: Confucius was a Chinese philosopher and politician who lived from 551 to 479 BCE. He was born in the state of Lu, which is now part of modern-day Shandong Province. Confucius taught people about being good citizens, having fair relationships with others, and doing what's right. He believed that education and self-improvement are very important for personal growth. His ideas had a big impact on East Asian thought and culture, and he's often credited with creating the philosophy of Confucianism. However, some scholars think he was more like a teacher who shared existing knowledge rather than an original thinker. His teachings were written down by his students in a book called the Analects, which is still widely read today. Confucius' ideas have influenced many aspects of society, including politics, education, and social norms not just in China but also in other parts of Asia.
label: llm

text: The scientific study of plants is called botany. It involves looking at how plants grow, develop, and reproduce, as well as their internal workings like photosynthesis and nutrient uptake. Botanists also examine the different types of plants, where they live, and how they interact with other living things in their environment. This field of study includes many areas of expertise, such as studying plant cells and molecules using special tools like microscopes and spectroscopes. Botany has many practical uses, including helping farmers grow crops more efficiently, developing new medicines from plants, and protecting endangered species. By understanding how plants work and interact with their surroundings, botanists can contribute to the development of new technologies and products derived from plants, making it a vital area of study in modern science.
label: llm

text: Arnulf fought wars against Great Moravia from 892 to 899 but was unable to fully conquer it. However, he did achieve some successes during this time. In 895, the Duchy of Bohemia broke away from Great Moravia and became a vassal state under Arnulf's control. This meant that Bohemia was no longer at risk of being invaded by the Franks. At around the same time, Great Moravia likely lost some territory to Arnulf, including parts of present-day western Hungary. As a reward for his efforts, Wiching became Arnulf's advisor in 892. In 899, Arnulf even asked for help from the Magyars who lived in the Carpathian Basin and with their assistance he was able to exert control over Moravia.

text: Babrak Karmal was an Afghan politician who played a key role in the country's government from 1979 to 1986. Born in 1929, he studied law at Kabul University before joining the People's Democratic Party of Afghanistan (PDPA) in the early 1960s. He quickly rose through the party ranks and became involved in the Saur Revolution that overthrew the monarchy in 1978. After the Soviet invasion of Afghanistan in 1979, Karmal was appointed leader of the PDPA and President by the Soviet-backed government. His time as President was marked by efforts to make the country more socialist and modernize its economy, but he faced opposition from groups who wanted a more traditional way of life. The Soviet Union supported his government with money and military aid, but Karmal eventually resigned in 1986 due to disagreements with the Soviets over how to fight against the mujahideen.
label: llm

text: Blizzard Entertainment is a well-known American video game developer and publisher based in California. Founded on February 8, 1991, by three people - Mike Morhaime, Frank Pearce, and Allen Adham - the company has become one of the most successful and influential gaming studios worldwide. At first, Blizzard's games didn't get much attention, but things changed with "Warcraft: Orcs & Humans" in 1994, a real-time strategy game that made people take notice. The studio then released more popular titles like "StarCraft" in 1998 and "Diablo II" in 2000, which helped establish Blizzard as a major player in the gaming industry. One of its most famous games is probably "World of Warcraft," an online multiplayer game released in 2004 that has become one of the best-selling video games ever made. Since then, Blizzard has continued to release new and popular games like "Overwatch" in 2016 and "Hearthstone" in 2014, solidifying its position as a leader in the gaming industry.
label: llm

text: Governments that are corrupt often give special treatment to certain business owners who have close connections with them. This can also happen based on religion or ethnicity. For example, in Syria, the Alawite minority has more influence over the government and businesses there, including President Assad himself being an Alawite. To understand this, think of people as connected through relationships like a social network. When leaders need help to achieve their goals, they often turn to others who have power and influence. These powerful individuals become central figures in the network. In many developing countries, these hubs are limited, which means that economic and political power is concentrated among a small group of interconnected people.
label: human
